definition: A mathematical algorithm that maps data of arbitrary size to a bit string of a fixed size (the hash value). It is designed to be a one-way function, collision-resistant, and deterministic. NIST defines it as a hash algorithm that can be used to generate digests of messages.

Secure Hash Algorithm 256-bit, a member of the SHA-2 family of cryptographic hash functions. It produces a 256-bit (32-byte) hash value, commonly represented as a 64-character hexadecimal string. SHA-2 is widely used for data integrity and digital signatures.

A property of cryptographic hash functions where it is computationally infeasible to find two different inputs that produce the same hash output. For SHA-256, the probability of a collision is astronomically low.

A digital record containing information about a product's lifecycle, circularity, and sustainability-related data. Mandated by regulations like the EU ESPR, DPPs aim to enhance transparency and traceability in supply chains.

The process of computing a cryptographic hash of a document or data directly on the user's local device before it is transmitted to a server. This approach enhances privacy by ensuring the original sensitive data never leaves the client's control.

The Electronic Communications and Transactions Act, 2002 (Act No. 25 of 2002) of South Africa. It provides legal recognition for electronic data, transactions, and signatures, and addresses the integrity of data messages.

The Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013 (Act No. 4 of 2013) of South Africa. It regulates the processing of personal information and mandates measures to ensure its integrity and confidentiality.

Also known as a hash tree, it is a tree-like data structure in which every leaf node is labeled with the cryptographic hash of a data block, and every non-leaf node is labeled with the hash of its child nodes. It allows for efficient and secure verification of large data sets. Merkle trees are fundamental to blockchain technology.

A cryptographic mechanism used to verify the authenticity and integrity of a digital message or document, and to provide non-repudiation. It combines hashing with asymmetric cryptography. Digital signatures are legally recognized in many jurisdictions.

An official document issued by the plant protection organization of an exporting country to the plant protection organization of an importing country. It certifies that plants, plant products, or other regulated articles meet specified phytosanitary import requirements. Phytosanitary certificates are crucial for international trade in agricultural products.
